title: Federal Reserve Board announces it has made the joint findings with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency required for the OCC to approve a request by Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A., for an exemption under section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act
summary: The Federal Reserve Board announced on March 26, 2026 that it has made the joint findings with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) required for the OCC to approve Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A.'s request for an exemption under Section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act. The exemption is sought to enable an internal corporate reorganization involving the bank's affiliate, Morgan Stanley Europe SE, headquartered in Frankfurt am Main, Germany.
key_points: Fed and OCC jointly made findings required for OCC to approve Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A.'s Section 23A exemption request
Section 23A establishes limits and imposes requirements on a bank's transactions with its affiliates
The exemption is needed for an internal corporate reorganization involving Morgan Stanley Europe SE in Frankfurt, Germany
The bank is Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A. of Salt Lake City, Utah
Vice Chair Philip N. Jefferson, Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle W. Bowman, Governor Michael S. Barr, and Governor Lisa D. Cook each issued statements
why_it_matters: This is a specific bank regulatory action regarding affiliate transaction rules. Section 23A exemptions are relatively rare and require careful Fed/OCC coordination. For market structure purposes, this demonstrates the regulatory framework governing bank-affiliate relationships and intercompany transactions. The reorganization of Morgan Stanley Europe SE may have operational implications for the bank's European activities.
market_structure_context: Section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act sets limits and requirements on bank transactions with affiliates. This exemption approval is part of the regulatory framework governing bank-affiliate relationships and internal corporate structures. The joint Fed-OCC action reflects coordination between banking regulators on cross-border reorganization matters involving Morgan Stanley Europe SE.
